_comment: Draft coordinator reference copy. Use conditional funding language only. Do not present this page as an authorization, invoice template, or guarantee of DDD Goods & Services approval.

meta.title: Service Scope Notes for Coordinators | Who's to Say ? Foundation
meta.description: Reference language for coordinators reviewing public creative project services, individualized creator projects, and public workshops. Funding requires individual review and prior approval.

hero.title: Service Scope Notes for Coordinators
hero.subtitle: Reference language for understanding our public creative project services, individualized creator projects, and public workshops. This page is informational only and does not guarantee funding approval.

whatWeProvide.title: What We Provide
whatWeProvide.intro: Who's to Say Foundation offers interest-driven creative project services and public workshops. We are not presenting this page as licensed day habilitation, clinical treatment, case management, job placement, personal training, or general life coaching.
items: Individualized creator projects in video, design, photography, audio, storytelling, and portfolio development
Scheduled small-group public workshops when offered and publicly available
Participant-specific technical implementation only when it is tied to an approved participant-owned project scope
Documentation of dates, format, session focus, participant-owned deliverables, and any separately approved technical implementation

sampleDescriptions.title: Individual Creator Project
sampleDescriptions.body: A scoped creative-media engagement supporting the person in a defined participant-owned project such as a short video, audio story, portfolio page, sports-media recap, design set, or presentation piece. The written scope should identify live session time, deliverables, and any participant-specific technical implementation.

sampleDescriptions.title: Digital Storytelling / Portfolio Session
sampleDescriptions.body: Participant-facing creative session focused on accessible video, audio, photography, design, or portfolio development. The purpose is a defined creative deliverable, not direct support, therapy, case management, job placement, or open-ended life coaching.

sampleDescriptions.title: Small-Group Public Workshop
sampleDescriptions.body: A scheduled public workshop with a defined topic, date, instructor, capacity, and enrollment terms. Public workshops should be distinguished from individualized creator projects and should not be used to re-label a 1:1 engagement as a class.

sampleDescriptions.title: Sports Media Story Project
sampleDescriptions.body: Creative-media project work connected to sports storytelling, such as interviews, recap videos, trading-card concepts, fan commentary, or event storytelling. Fitness instruction, personal training, conditioning plans, admission fees, supervision, and unrelated entertainment are outside this creative-service scope.

invoiceDescriptions.title: Quote / SOW requirement
invoiceDescriptions.body: Use a participant-specific quote or statement of work before services begin. It should name the service, dates, rate, session length, deliverables, materials, implementation cap, cancellation terms, and funding source requirements.

invoiceDescriptions.title: Documentation boundary
invoiceDescriptions.body: Do not document outside technical implementation as live participant-facing session time. Do not bill Foundation overhead, reusable templates, staff training, fundraising assets, or general infrastructure to an individual participant.

documentation.title: Documentation Available
items: Session date, start/end time, duration, and format
Participant-facing work completed during the session
Separately approved participant-specific technical implementation, when applicable
Materials, third-party costs, and follow-up recommendations when included in the quote

fundingNote.title: Funding & Approval Boundary
fundingNote.body: Funding is not guaranteed. Any DDD, plan-based, or third-party-funded purchase must match the individual's documented need and must be approved in writing before services begin. The approval should identify the exact service, dates, rate, units, participant-facing time, any participant-specific technical implementation, and deliverables. When the work is primarily direct support, life skills, community accompaniment, employment support, therapy, case management, or personal training, it should be routed to the appropriate approved service lane or provider partner instead of being presented as a public creative service.

nextPhase.title: Next-Phase Vision
nextPhase.body: We are building toward an expanded maker space and community studio — a future phase supported by fundraising, not a current licensed facility. Public language should reflect creative services and community storytelling, not day-program, clinical, direct-support, or residential-service claims.

sidebar.contactIntro: Email or call with scope, documentation, privacy, consent, or scheduling questions.
quickReferenceItems: Public creative services, not open-ended life coaching
Direct support, life skills, employment support, therapy, case management, and personal training require another lane
sidebar.fundingCardBody: Use conditional phrasing only: "may be eligible," "requires individual review," "subject to prior written approval," and "only within the approved scope."

finalCta.title: Questions about a specific participant?
finalCta.body: Reach out with the person's goals, proposed deliverable, funding source, timeline, and documentation requirements so the right lane can be identified before services begin.
